description: Canonical field definitions for ENL node types. All nodes conforming to ENL-002 must satisfy this schema.

node_id.description: Unique identifier for this node within the graph. Must not be shared across nodes in the same graph.

node_type.description: Canonical ENL node type. Determines allowed layer position and derivation rules.

run_id.description: Execution context identifier. All linked nodes in a graph must share the same run_id in v1.

title.description: Short human-readable label for the node. Used for navigation display only.

status.description: Current lifecycle status of the node.

derived_from.description: Ordered list of upstream node_ids this node depends on. Empty array is only valid for EVID nodes. All referenced node_ids must exist in the same graph.
constraints: empty only permitted for EVID node_type
all values must be resolvable node_ids in the same graph
all referenced nodes must belong to the correct upstream layer type

source_ref.description: Canonical reference to the source artifact this node is grounded in. Must point to a declared artifact path or identifier.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 date or timestamp indicating when this node artifact was created.

status_values.defined: Node declared but not yet active in an execution context.
status_values.active: Node is part of an active execution run.
status_values.validated: Node has passed all required validation checks.
status_values.blocked: Node cannot proceed due to upstream dependency failure or missing evidence.
status_values.rejected: Node has been explicitly rejected and must not be used in navigation paths.

v1_constraints: All fields in required_fields are mandatory for every node.
No additional fields are required by v1 beyond those declared here.
Optional fields may be added by consuming systems but must not replace required fields.
Cross-run derivation is not permitted in v1.
Same-layer derivation is not permitted in v1.
